q.en: On what date was the Gettysburg Address delivered?
q.zh: 葛底斯堡演说是在哪一天发表的？

options.en: November 19, 1863, at the dedication of the Soldiers' National Cemetery.
options.zh: 1863年11月19日，在士兵国家公墓的落成典礼上。

options.en: January 1, 1863.
April 9, 1865.

explain.en: The book states the cemetery was dedicated on November 19, 1863, the day Lincoln gave the address.
explain.zh: 书中说公墓于1863年11月19日落成，那一天林肯发表了演说。

q.en: Who was the main speaker at the dedication ceremony, and how long did he speak?
q.zh: 典礼上的主要演讲者是谁，他讲了多久？

options.en: Edward Everett spoke for about two hours; Lincoln spoke only about two minutes.
options.zh: 爱德华·埃弗里特讲了约两小时；林肯只讲了大约两分钟。

options.en: Abraham Lincoln spoke for two hours.
options.zh: 亚伯拉罕·林肯讲了两个小时。

options.en: Thomas Jefferson gave the main speech.
options.zh: 托马斯·杰斐逊作了主要演讲。

options.en: No one else spoke besides Lincoln.
options.zh: 除了林肯没有别人讲话。

explain.en: Edward Everett gave the formal two-hour speech, while Lincoln was asked for 'a few appropriate remarks' and spoke only two minutes.
explain.zh: 爱德华·埃弗里特作了正式的、长达两小时的演讲，而林肯只被请说'几句得体的话'，仅讲了约两分钟。

q.en: When Lincoln said 'four score and seven years ago,' what year was he referring to?
q.zh: 林肯说'八十七年前'（four score and seven years ago）指的是哪一年？

options.en: 1776, the year of the Declaration of Independence.
1863, the year of the speech.
1787, when the Constitution was written.
1619, the first colonies.

explain.en: The book explains four score and seven (87) years before 1863 is 1776, the year of the Declaration of Independence.
explain.zh: 书中解释1863年的87年前是1776年，即《独立宣言》发表之年。

q.en: Which famous phrase closes the Gettysburg Address?
q.zh: 葛底斯堡演说以哪句著名的话作结？

options.en: 'government of the people, by the people, for the people, shall not perish from the earth.'
options.zh: '民有、民治、民享的政府，永远不会从地球上消亡。'

options.en: 'give me liberty or give me death.'
'I have a dream.'
'ask not what your country can do for you.'

explain.en: The book highlights the closing line about a government 'of the people, by the people, for the people.'
explain.zh: 书中强调了结尾那句关于'民有、民治、民享的政府'的话。

q.en: Why is the Gettysburg Address considered so important?
q.zh: 为什么葛底斯堡演说被认为如此重要？

options.en: It was the longest speech ever given.
In just ten sentences it summarized the war and the spirit of a nation, inspiring later generations.
options.zh: 它仅用十句话就概括了战争与国家的精神，激励了后世。

options.en: It ended the Civil War the next day.
options.zh: 它在第二天就结束了内战。

options.en: It was written by Edward Everett.
options.zh: 它由爱德华·埃弗里特撰写。

explain.en: The book says in just ten sentences Lincoln summarized the war and the spirit of a nation, and Americans have been inspired by it ever since.
explain.zh: 书中说林肯仅用十句话就概括了战争与国家的精神，美国人从此一直受其激励。

q.en: How many versions of the speech exist, and which is most often reproduced?
q.zh: 这份演说共有多少个版本，哪一个最常被人翻印？

options.en: Five versions exist in Lincoln's own hand; the fifth (signed) is the most reproduced.
options.zh: 共有林肯亲笔的五个版本；第五个（有签名）最常被翻印。

options.en: Only one version exists.
Ten versions exist, all unsigned.
options.zh: 存在十个版本，都未签名。

options.en: Two versions exist, both lost.
options.zh: 存在两个版本，都已遗失。

explain.en: The book notes five copies exist in Lincoln's own hand, all slightly different; the fifth and last, which he signed, is most reproduced.
explain.zh: 书中说现存林肯亲笔的五个版本，彼此略有不同；第五个也即最后一个由他签名，最常被人翻印。

q.en: What illness did Lincoln come down with around the time of the speech?
q.zh: 在演说前后，林肯染上了什么疾病？

options.en: Smallpox.
The flu.
A broken leg.
Pneumonia.

explain.en: The book says Lincoln was coming down with a serious illness and was later diagnosed with smallpox.
explain.zh: 书中说林肯当时正染上重病，后来被诊断为天花。

q.en: According to the book, why did Lincoln say the ground was already sacred before his words?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，为什么林肯说这片土地在他的讲话之前就已经神圣？

options.en: Because the government declared it holy by law.
options.zh: 因为政府以法律宣布它为圣地。

options.en: Because the brave soldiers, living and dead, had consecrated it far above his power to add or detract.
options.zh: 因为那些勇敢的、活着与牺牲的士兵早已使它神圣，远非他的言辞所能增减。

options.en: Because it was built on a church.
options.zh: 因为它建在一座教堂之上。

options.en: Because Edward Everett blessed it.
options.zh: 因为爱德华·埃弗里特为它祝圣。

explain.en: Lincoln said the brave men, living and dead, had consecrated the ground far above our poor power to add or detract.
explain.zh: 林肯说那些勇敢的、活着与牺牲的士兵早已使这片土地神圣，远非我们微薄的力量所能增添或减损。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To explain the Gettysburg Address and why it is one of the most important speeches in American history.
options.zh: 解释葛底斯堡演说，以及它为何是美国历史上最重要的演说之一。

options.en: To argue that Edward Everett was the better speaker.
options.zh: 论证爱德华·埃弗里特是更好的演讲者。

options.en: To teach readers how to write poetry.
To describe the life of Robert E. Lee.

explain.en: The book's focus question asks why the address is one of the most important speeches, and it explains the text line by line.
explain.zh: 本书的焦点问题就是'为什么葛底斯堡演说是美国最重要的演说之一'，并逐句解释了演说词。

q.en: In the address, what does Lincoln mean by 'conceived in Liberty'?
q.zh: 在演说中，林肯所说的'conceived in Liberty'（孕育于自由之中）里，conceived 是什么意思？

options.en: Thought of or imagined.
Destroyed.
Sold.
Measured.

explain.en: The glossary defines 'conceived' as 'thought of; imagined,' as when the Founders brought forth a new nation.
explain.zh: 词汇表把 conceived 定义为'构想出、想象出'，正如建国者缔造一个新国家那样。

prompt.en: Lincoln said 'the world will little note, nor long remember what we say here, but it can never forget what they did here.' Do you think he was right? Why do you think his short speech is still remembered today while longer speeches are forgotten?
prompt.zh: 林肯说：'世界不会太注意，也不会长久记住我们在此说的话，但它绝不会忘记他们在此做的事。'你认为他说得对吗？为什么他这简短的演说至今仍被铭记，而更长的演说却被遗忘？

en: Think about the book's point that actions speak louder than words, and that Lincoln honored the fallen soldiers.
Consider why a ten-sentence speech summarizing a nation's spirit might outlast a two-hour speech.
zh: 想想书中'行动胜于言辞'的观点，以及林肯对阵亡将士的致敬。
思考为什么一篇概括国家精神的十句话演说，可能比两小时的演讲流传更久。
